title: rosa canina subvar. euoxyphylla
intro: Rosa canina subvar. euoxyphylla, also known as the dog rose, is a subvariety of the Rosa canina plant, which is a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina subvar. euoxyphylla include its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and carotenoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to support cardiovascular health, improve vision, and aid in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Rosa canina subvar. euoxyphylla can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to promote overall health and well-being.
